commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OSPF SR TLV parsing and database detail display

- Move SidLabelTlv to packet-utils crate for sharing between isis-packet and ospf-packet
- Add OSPF-style SID/Label Sub-TLV parser (2+2 byte header vs IS-IS 1+1)
- Parse RouterInfo TLVs: SID/Label Range, Local Block, Router Capability, Algorithm
- Implement Extended Prefix Opaque LSA parser (RFC 7684) with Prefix SID Sub-TLV (RFC 8665)
- Add "show ip ospf database detail" output for Opaque Area LSAs with SR details
- Clean up debug println statements in nfsm.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fill triangular gaps in Case 3 split-face extrude

When extruding a split face (Case 3: mixed moved/duplicated vertices),
non_coplanar edges were skipped for wall creation. But when one vertex
of such an edge is moved and the other duplicated, a triangular gap
appears. Now all edges with shifted endpoints get walls, with
deduplication to collapse quads with repeated vertices into triangles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: sistema de nurturing con recordatorios automaticos y panel admin

Implementa flujo completo de nurturing: 3 modelos (NurturingConfig,
NurturingPlantilla, NurturingLog), controlador admin con dashboard,
configuracion editable, CRUD de plantillas con preview, gestion de
contactos con acciones individuales/masivas, cron independiente de
recordatorios, desuscripcion publica con token, e integracion con
el panel de mensajes existente.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): fix HEMesh corruption from recycled half-edge IDs

When addFace consumed a boundary half-edge, it tried to unlink it from
the boundary chain by following stale next/prev pointers. After ID
recycling via the free list, those pointers could reference completely
different (reused) half-edges. Setting their next/prev to NONE corrupted
face loops, causing faceLoop to crash with "Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'next')" on subsequent extrude operations.

Fix: verify the neighbor actually references BACK to the boundary HE
before modifying it. If the ID was recycled, the bidirectional check
fails and we skip the (corrupting) unlink.

Adds regression test for stair-building sequence:
box → split → extrude → split → extrude.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: update FCC address collection and add fcc-ip-doc-updater agent

Add fcc-ip-doc-updater agent for incremental FCC address updates from
GitHub issue #5 community feedback, with strict deletion policy and
address ordering rules. Update FCC address docs with 8 new addresses
(Zhejiang Wenzhou 4, Hunan Shaoyang 1, Henan Unicom 2, Hebei Telecom 1).

Add polish mode two-pane review UI with full-document rewrite

Replace single-panel DocumentPanel for polish mode with a two-column
layout showing original draft (left) vs Claude's suggested revision
(right). Claude rewrites the entire document using a [REWRITE] format,
then walks the user through each paragraph one at a time with
accept/reject/edit controls and auto-advance for unchanged paragraphs.

- Create PolishPanel.jsx with two-column review, progress bar, and
  review control bar (accept/reject/edit per paragraph)
- Add parseRewrite() and parseStreamingRewrite() to proposal-parser.js
- Add [REWRITE] streaming detection in ClaudeCompose.jsx
- Add polish state (revisions, reviewIndex, complete) and handlers
- Update handlePolishSubmit with full-rewrite prompt
- Add upload timing note inline on DraftPasteOverlay
- Increase max_tokens from 4096 to 8192 for full-document rewrites
- Add [REWRITE] format docs to system prompt

Padding adjustments across breakpoints:
- Mobile: reduced from 40px to 10px top padding
- Tablet: reduced from 80px to 50px top padding
- Desktop: reduced from 80px to 50px top padding
- Moves buttons ~30px higher to match original (y=505 → y=475)

Card image overlap improvements:
- Base: increased from -180px to -220px margin-top
- Desktop: increased from -200px to -260px margin-top

These adjustments bring the hero section to near pixel-perfect alignment
with the original design across all viewports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: live keyboard resize — real-time re-layout during drag

Replace setScaleY() visual stretch with throttled reloadMainKeyboard()
during drag, so keys rebuild at correct proportions in real-time.
Same proven approach as HeliBoard's one-handed mode resize.

- Remove all setScaleY()/setPivotY() calls from resize mode
- Add 120ms throttle to prevent excessive rebuilds during drag
- Add resizeOverlayToKeyboard() to sync overlay size after rebuild
- Replace setThemeNeedsReload() (hide/show window) with direct
  reloadMainKeyboard() — no more flicker or keyboard dismissal
- Fix: keyboard no longer hides during resize drag

commit.message:
feat: custom emoji reaction roles + role settings fixes + 3-state permissions

Phase A: Fix is_hoisted and is_mentionable role settings that existed in
DB/schema but were silently ignored by the PATCH handler. Add "Mentionable"
toggle to role settings UI.

Phase B: Add custom emoji support for reaction roles. Default role groups
now use custom emojis from imported packs (membership-tiers, regions,
gaming-platforms, tiktok-faces, etc.) with unicode fallback. Includes DB
migration, emoji picker in admin UI, and enriched API responses.

Phase C: 3-state permission toggles (Allow/Deny/Default) for role
permissions. Adds deny permission columns to roles table, position-ordered
permission calculation where higher-position roles override lower ones,
and a cycling 3-state toggle UI replacing the old boolean switches.

test(engine): add law mining bundle write and summary render tests

Adds two integration tests for the law mining artifact infrastructure:

- default_bundle_writes_required_artifacts: creates an ArtifactContext
  with a temp dir, calls emit_default_law_mining_bundle(), and asserts
  all 13 artifact paths exist (catalog, seeds, hypotheses, provenance,
  scope, trace IDs, manifest, events, commands, env, artifact index,
  repro lock, summary); also deserializes the run manifest and verifies
  schema version, bead ID, and artifact hash presence
- render_summary_mentions_top_candidate: builds a catalog from the
  default fixture and asserts the summary markdown contains the expected
  heading and candidate_id field

Also adds test-module imports (fs, PathBuf, SystemTime) and a temp_dir()
helper with nanosecond nonce for collision-free test isolation.

Also updates beads issue tracking state.

commit.message:
Fix Oktagon live tracker: cancellation detection, concurrent events, result fallbacks

Three bugs fixed:
- Cancellation detection skipped COMPLETED fights, so lifecycle-completed
  fights (no winner) like Wagner vs Kalejaiye were never cancelled even
  when missing from scraped data. Now only skips COMPLETED fights that
  have a real winner.
- stripDiacritics mismatch between scraped and DB fight signatures caused
  false cancellations for Czech/Slovak fighter names.
- findFirst in Step 1.5 only dispatched one workflow; when UFC and Oktagon
  were both LIVE, Oktagon tracker was starved. Changed to findMany with
  per-workflow cooldown so both run independently.

Also added fallback result detection (fight.winner field, fighter isWinner
flags, DRAW/NC handling) and unknown format logging in the scraper.

commit.message:
Add Evm64 arithmetic: ADD and SUB with parallel build

Split into 3 files for build parallelism:
- Arithmetic.lean: programs + per-limb specs (shared base)
- Add.lean: full 256-bit ADD composition (30 instrs, 4-limb carry chain)
- Sub.lean: full 256-bit SUB composition (30 instrs, 4-limb borrow chain)

Add and Sub build in parallel (~23s each vs ~194s sequential for 32-bit).

commit.message:
Fix launch: STATIC body during placement + recompute COM from actual pos

Two fixes:
1. Set structureBody.type = STATIC during placement (not just zero
   velocity). STATIC bodies don't integrate at all — zero drift.
2. In addCubeShape, reverse-engineer _comLocal from the body's actual
   world position instead of using the stale value from createStructureBody.
   This guarantees the shape offset matches reality even if the body
   settled before placement started. v2.1.1

fix: prevent shell injection in auto-version workflow (#117)

Pass commit message via environment variable instead of direct
interpolation in the Detect bump type step. Direct interpolation
of ${{ github.event.head_commit.message }} breaks when the commit
message contains shell metacharacters (quotes, backticks, $, etc.),
causing exit code 127.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HfVit14qTjbCs8futEfe4X
